description_long: This object is a stylized, blocky airplane reminiscent of a World War II-era military aircraft, constructed with a pixelated, voxel-like design. The fuselage is primarily light blue with darker blue and gray accents, and it features a glass-like cockpit at the front. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, each bearing a roundel insignia and the letters 'USA' on the right wing, with red, white, and blue striping on the tail fin and horizontal stabilizers. The airplane has two visible engines mounted under the wings, each with a propeller, and a tricycle landing gear configuration with two main wheels and a nose wheel. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers. The overall appearance suggests a toy or model airplane, likely made from interlocking plastic bricks.
description: A blocky, light blue military-style airplane with mid-mounted straight wings, twin propeller engines, and a conventional striped tail. Features include a glass-like cockpit, roundel insignia, and tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full fuselage, cockpit, propeller engine, and roundel insignia on the wing.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the straight wings, 'USA' lettering, roundel insignia, and twin engines.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other engine, cockpit, and tail striping.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail's striped design, horizontal stabilizers, and the back of the engines.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible dihedral or winglets, featuring roundel insignia.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted under each wing, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers, all with striped markings.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, fixed configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a transparent canopy, likely single-seat.
